Alrighty, let's rock and roll. So the two types of traffic for organic slash unpaid media. All right, so let's dig in. All right, so these are the people who follow you already. They already have some degree of connection to you. Doesn't really mean that they know you, but they know of you or they've friended you at some point or they like something or they follow you or whatever. All right, four, and so the objective here is that we're trying to get opt-ins. We're trying to get people who opt-in to working with us, meaning that they give us their contact info so that we can reach out to them beyond this social network. So in Instagram, this would be anyone who comments on your post or likes your post. So let's walk through this. So I'd go in here and I would click who is like my post and then I would click on their name and then I would message them. Oops, not that one. And then I would message them. Where's my damn messenger? Normally you can message people. What am I missing here? That's probably because I'm on a computer. All right, but when you're on here, then you would message that person. Very straightforward. Now, next thing, if I'm going back here, let's go back. Now let's see, so that was the people who liked my post here. The next one would be, let's look at all my comments. All right, same thing here. God, that's annoying. All right, then we go reach out to the comment people and then do the same thing. We're gonna message them as well. So comments are gonna be obviously the first priority and then the secondary priority is gonna be the people who liked your posts. And that's why posting on a regular basis is a good way to generate some interest. Make sure that what you're providing is valuable and breaks a belief more than anything. All right, people go on Instagram to be entertained and ultimately to feel an emotion. And so what you wanna evoke with any of the content you have is in a motion that someone can relate to. Whether that's happy, sad, glad, motivation, whatever it is, you actually have to evoke a feeling. That is the whole goal of any content that you put out. So that is on Instagram for warm traffic. Anyone who comments or likes on your posts, you're gonna message them. On Facebook, it would be the same way. So if I made a post, which I don't make posts that much, because I don't really, we have a very big company. So I don't really want people to message me. But if I made a post, right, this is a whole bunch of other people who are posting all about my stuff, but then you just go, you'd message these people, right? Message them and then find all the people who liked your stuff and you'd message them too. All right, it's right there. Very straightforward and then you'd start the conversation with them. All right, now, we covered these first two, boom, boom. And then same thing on LinkedIn. I won't go do it again, so I don't wanna bore you. Remember, speed to money. Anyone who comments on your posts or likes your posts that you have on LinkedIn, you wanna directly reach out to them. And this is warm traffic, right? This is like the warmest traffic. This is the most likely people who are going to buy. So that's why having a fresh amount of content that goes out is good. From a strategy standpoint, so you can have people take micro commitments and take steps towards you, which decreases the amount of time you have to sell. All right? And then I will go through cold traffic. Well, yolo, let's do it. So cold traffic, the best way to do this is searching through hashtags. So if you're on here, we go over here, and we go hashtag fit mom, right? Or fit moms, or fit mom life, right? Any of these things are fine. Like you literally just work these freaking fit mom ones, Now, when you go here, important things, I think it's on your phone. There you go. You wanna go to most recent, not top. Top is gonna be like celebrities and shit, and you're not gonna wanna mess with them. But you wanna look at most recent, and you wanna look for people who look like they need help, right? Now, if you see somebody who's super fit, then you might not wanna work with this girl, cause she seems like she's really fit, right? But maybe this girl isn't super, super fit. Let's see, if she's a coach, see, so she's a coach, right? So she's already trying to monetize her stuff, so you don't wanna work with her, right? That's a waste of time, so don't worry about them. But you wanna keep going down here and trying to find people who look like they need help, right? So we get fit journey, right? So now we might have some more people who look like they do, do, do, do, do. You just wanna find normal folks, right? This guy looks normal, doesn't look like he's doing anything special. Okay, maybe he wants help, right? You reach out to him, all right? This is how it works, it's a numbers game. So you're gonna search through hashtags, and remember, you're not looking at top posts, you're looking at recent posts. Where is my damn recent posts? There it is, you're looking at recent posts, cause these are gonna be the most normal people who are there, and you don't even have to do that. All right, for Instagram, which tends to be the best one in my opinion, cause it's easier to search through them, versus Facebook, where you kind of have to go through groups, which is a little bit more challenging. So I would recommend you start with Instagram as your primary method of communicating with folks.
